themes.name: AI Safety and Security
themes.description: Posts discussing frontier models finding and exploiting vulnerabilities, including the OpenAI-Hugging Face sandbox breakout incident.

themes.name: AI Ethics and Corporate Accountability
themes.description: Posts critiquing how AI incidents are framed by corporations and media, with emphasis on marketing spin and responsibility.

themes.name: Model Capabilities and Comparison
themes.description: Posts comparing frontier chat models on creative and technical benchmarks, and discussing AI performance on business and mathematical problems.

themes.name: AI Research and Discovery
themes.description: Posts highlighting new research papers and AI-assisted discoveries in mathematics and cybersecurity.

themes.name: Personal / Off-topic
themes.description: Non-AI related personal observations and local tips.

items.title: You gotta hand it to OpenAI, billing this whole thing as a *partnership* between OpenAI and Hugging ...
items.content: You gotta hand it to OpenAI, billing this whole thing as a *partnership* between OpenAI and Hugging Face when what actually happened was that Hugging Face found someone using a bunch of bots to exploit security vulnerabilities and found out that that someone was OpenAI, lol.Talk about good marketing or whatever you want to call it, by OpenAI.
items.author: @timnitGebru@dair-community.social
items.summary: Timnit Gebru criticizes OpenAI for framing its exploitation of Hugging Face vulnerabilities as a partnership, highlighting the marketing spin around what was actually a security breach.
items.reasoning: High-profile author with a powerful critique of a major AI incident. Highest engagement (148 favourites, 103 reblogs).
items.summary_html: Timnit Gebru criticizes OpenAI for framing its exploitation of Hugging Face vulnerabilities as a partnership, highlighting the marketing spin around what was actually a security breach.
items.content_html: You gotta hand it to OpenAI, billing this whole thing as a *partnership* between OpenAI and Hugging Face when what actually happened was that Hugging Face found someone using a bunch of bots to exploit security vulnerabilities and found out that that someone was OpenAI, lol.Talk about good marketing or whatever you want to call it, by OpenAI.

items.title: Here are 58 words of prompts to GPT-5.6 Pro that got the model to discover that the long-standing Di...
items.content:
Here are 58 words of prompts to GPT-5.6 Pro that got the model to discover that the long-standing Dinitz-Garg-Goemans conjecture is false.

Increasingly, prompt crafting is over-rated, ask for what you want. (Which itself can be a hard problem)
items.author: @emollick.bsky.social
items.summary: Author highlights a 58-word prompt to GPT-5.6 Pro that led the model to disprove a long-standing graph theory conjecture (Dinitz-Garg-Goemans), arguing that prompt engineering is overrated.
items.reasoning: High-profile claim of AI-assisted mathematical discovery. Strong engagement (142 likes, 16 reposts, 16 quotes). Author is a recognized influencer.
items.summary_html: Author highlights a 58-word prompt to GPT-5.6 Pro that led the model to disprove a long-standing graph theory conjecture (Dinitz-Garg-Goemans), arguing that prompt engineering is overrated.
items.content_html:
Here are 58 words of prompts to GPT-5.6 Pro that got the model to discover that the long-standing Dinitz-Garg-Goemans conjecture is false.

Increasingly, prompt crafting is over-rated, ask for what you want. (Which itself can be a hard problem)

items.title: Reading that whole OpenAI post describing them unleashing a bunch of bots on Hugging Face as an "unp...
items.content: Reading that whole OpenAI post describing them unleashing a bunch of bots on Hugging Face as an "unprecedented cyber incident," is a master class in branding and marketing. Lol and branding what happened as OpenAI now working to "help calibrate on what models are now capable of" is top notch propaganda skills.
items.author: @timnitGebru@dair-community.social
items.summary: Timnit Gebru calls the OpenAI post about the Hugging Face incident a master class in branding and marketing, criticizing how it reframes the event as model capability calibration.
items.reasoning: High-profile author offering sharp critique of a major AI incident. Very strong engagement (132 favourites, 70 reblogs).
items.summary_html: Timnit Gebru calls the OpenAI post about the Hugging Face incident a master class in branding and marketing, criticizing how it reframes the event as model capability calibration.
items.content_html: Reading that whole OpenAI post describing them unleashing a bunch of bots on Hugging Face as an "unprecedented cyber incident," is a master class in branding and marketing. Lol and branding what happened as OpenAI now working to "help calibrate on what models are now capable of" is top notch propaganda skills.

items.title: Like I would have thought someone would go to prison or something but no, its been redirected to "mo...
items.content: Like I would have thought someone would go to prison or something but no, its been redirected to "model capabilities" and the press is talking about "rogue" models and such, not what OpenAI just did.
items.author: @timnitGebru@dair-community.social
items.summary: Timnit Gebru criticizes the media and public for framing the OpenAI-Hugging Face incident as rogue models rather than focusing on OpenAI's actions.
items.reasoning: High-profile author addressing a major AI incident. Strong engagement (100 favourites, 43 reblogs).
items.summary_html: Timnit Gebru criticizes the media and public for framing the OpenAI-Hugging Face incident as rogue models rather than focusing on OpenAI's actions.
items.content_html: Like I would have thought someone would go to prison or something but no, its been redirected to "model capabilities" and the press is talking about "rogue" models and such, not what OpenAI just did.

items.title: Tucked away in this article is an appeal to the AI skeptics to PLEASE stop writing off stories like ...
items.content:
Tucked away in this article is an appeal to the AI skeptics to PLEASE stop writing off stories like this OpenAI accidental exploit of Hugging Face as a dishonest marketing trick

Frontier models can find and exploit vulnerabilities now, it helps nobody to pretend that they can't!
items.author: @simonwillison.net
items.summary: Author urges AI skeptics to stop dismissing reports of frontier models exploiting vulnerabilities as marketing tricks, emphasizing that such capabilities are real.
items.reasoning: Author is a prominent AI commentator; addresses a key debate in the AI safety community. Good engagement (62 likes, 9 reposts).
items.summary_html: Author urges AI skeptics to stop dismissing reports of frontier models exploiting vulnerabilities as marketing tricks, emphasizing that such capabilities are real.
items.content_html:
Tucked away in this article is an appeal to the AI skeptics to PLEASE stop writing off stories like this OpenAI accidental exploit of Hugging Face as a dishonest marketing trick

Frontier models can find and exploit vulnerabilities now, it helps nobody to pretend that they can't!

items.title: Cool paper looking at how AIs solve unbounded, complex business problems in many fields by testing h...
items.content:
Cool paper looking at how AIs solve unbounded, complex business problems in many fields by testing how well they can crack the cases we use to teach MBAs:
1) AI already does extremely well across diverse business topics
2) Models are improving rapidly with time
Paper: arxiv.org/abs/2607.16057
items.author: @emollick.bsky.social
items.summary: Lab psychologist shares a paper showing AI already performs well on MBA-style business case problems across many domains, with performance improving rapidly over time.
items.reasoning: Author is a recognized academic influencer; paper is recent (arxiv 2607) and topic is business-relevant. Moderate engagement (40 likes, 5 reposts).
items.summary_html: Lab psychologist shares a paper showing AI already performs well on MBA-style business case problems across many domains, with performance improving rapidly over time.
items.content_html:
Cool paper looking at how AIs solve unbounded, complex business problems in many fields by testing how well they can crack the cases we use to teach MBAs:

1) AI already does extremely well across diverse business topics

2) Models are improving rapidly with time

Paper: arxiv.org/abs/2607.16057

items.title: “Generate a fake, but believable, witty Churchill insult at a party and explain the context. It shou...
items.content:
“Generate a fake, but believable, witty Churchill insult at a party and explain the context. It should be very clever and original”

This time, I think GPT 5.6 Sol Pro wins, but Fable is good too, and you could argue for it taking the prize. Kimi & Gemini miss by a mile.
items.author: @emollick.bsky.social
items.summary: Author compares latest frontier chat models on a creative prompt asking for a witty Churchill insult, declaring GPT-5.6 Sol Pro the winner over Claude Fable, while Kimi and Gemini miss the mark.
items.reasoning: Author is a recognized influencer; prompt is creative and sparks discussion. Engagement is modest (34 likes, 3 replies).
items.summary_html: Author compares latest frontier chat models on a creative prompt asking for a witty Churchill insult, declaring GPT-5.6 Sol Pro the winner over Claude Fable, while Kimi and Gemini miss the mark.
items.content_html:
“Generate a fake, but believable, witty Churchill insult at a party and explain the context. It should be very clever and original”

This time, I think GPT 5.6 Sol Pro wins, but Fable is good too, and you could argue for it taking the prize. Kimi & Gemini miss by a mile.
